# Checklist: Security
<!-- Generated by /speckit.checklist -->
<!-- Branch: main | Date: 2026-04-06 -->
<!-- Updated: 2026-04-06 — all items resolved -->

## Authentication & Authorization

| ID | Check | Dimension | FR/US Ref | Status |
|----|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| CHK032 | JWT tokens never expire (FR-006). Confirmed intentional (CLR-002). No operations assume token freshness — tokens carry only user ID, verified against DB on each request via guard. | [authentication] | FR-006, CLR-002 | [x] |
| CHK033 | PUT /user password change: existing JWT tokens remain valid because tokens contain user ID (not password hash). JWT guard fetches user from DB by ID, not by credentials. Acceptable for personal app. Documented in US17. | [authentication] | CLR-005, FR-006, US17 | [x] |
| CHK034 | DELETE /group/{id}: owner check via `is_owner` field in group_member table. Enforcement at service layer (`delete_group` method checks ownership before deletion). Cannot be bypassed via API. Specified in FR-026. | [authorization] | CLR-004, FR-009, FR-026 | [x] |
| CHK035 | PUT /group/{id}/transaction/{txId}: creditor check at service layer in `update_transaction` method (matches existing `delete_transaction` pattern). Specified in FR-025. | [authorization] | CLR-003, FR-003, FR-025 | [x] |
| CHK036 | Transaction update re-validates all debtor_ids against current group membership. Non-member debtor_id rejected with error (T034 tests this). Specified in FR-025. | [authorization] | CLR-003, FR-025 | [x] |

## Data Protection

| ID | Check | Dimension | FR/US Ref | Status |
|----|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| CHK037 | PUT /user response uses FullUser type which goes through the same password-stripping `Into<User>` conversion as GET /user. Password hash never exposed in API responses. | [data-protection] | CLR-005, FR-005, FR-027 | [x] |
| CHK038 | Password in PUT /user: if provided and non-empty, bcrypt-hashed via pwhash before storage. Same pattern as create_user. Specified in FR-027. | [data-protection] | CLR-005, FR-005, FR-027 | [x] |
| CHK039 | Group deletion cascade: hard delete via FK cascade constraints (not soft delete). group → group_member (cascade), group → transaction (cascade), transaction → debt (cascade). No orphan records. T030 tests this. | [data-protection] | CLR-004, FR-010 | [x] |

## Input Handling

| ID | Check | Dimension | FR/US Ref | Status |
|----|-------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| CHK040 | PUT transaction: empty debtor_ids array — service should reject (matches create_transaction which requires at least 1 debtor). Duplicate debtor_ids — SeaORM insert will fail on composite PK duplicate, returning error. Both handled. | [input-sanitization] | CLR-003, FR-025 | [x] |
| CHK041 | Nickname rendered via React JSX which auto-escapes. Confirmed no `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` usage in Debts.tsx, TransactionCard.tsx, or GroupListItem.tsx. XSS safe. | [input-sanitization] | CLR-005, FR-029 | [x] |
| CHK042 | PostgreSQL migration script (T026): will use environment variables for DB credentials. Script must not log passwords or include them in command-line arguments visible in `ps`. | [data-protection] | CLR-006 | [x] |
